text:
Once upon an autumn evening by the hearth lived a peculiar fox named Fibbie.

Fobbily loved stories more than anything else. Especially stories about cunning foxes. One particular tale stayed with him.

There was a big owl who took great pleasure in swooping down on unsuspecting animals. Every year during autumn, Fobbily heard that owl delighting in crunching bones. He decided he wouldn’t give that owl the satisfaction.

First, fibbled some nuts that wouldn’t make interesting owl breakfast conversation. Then he listened closely to the owlor’s nighttime escapades. After a few moons, with nothing scary happening, the owl grew bored.

One night, hearing just the tattle tale of a small rabbit, Fobbily swooped down. "Why?

The little bunny just looked up innocently.

“Did you hear something?”

“There.”

“Oh. I wasn’t scared.”

“Oh?”

Just then, a big old hawk swooped down.

“Like that.”

Fobbily laughed
